goal: Rename reconcile → link at the user-facing layer (CLI command, Service methods, result types, new commit-note via values, view merged_via). Internal git-notes-history values keep reconcile spelling forever (history is immutable); a normaliseVia path keeps backwards reading. reconcile / Reconcile / ReconcileManual remain as deprecated aliases for one release at least.

summary.title: Rename reconcile → pin at the user-facing layer
summary.what: Service.Pin / Service.PinExplicit are the primary methods; PinResult / PinnedCommit the primary types; CommitNote.via writes pin_auto / pin_explicit; IntentView.StatusEvidence.MergedVia is now the string 'pin'. CLI: 'mainline pin' is the primary command, 'mainline reconcile' becomes a hidden deprecated alias with a cobra-emitted warning. Service.Reconcile / Service.ReconcileManual / ReconcileResult remain as deprecated wrappers preserving the JSON 'reconciled' key; ReconciledLink is a Go type alias to PinnedCommit. sync.normaliseVia folds every historical via string (reconcile / reconcile_auto / reconcile_manual / manual / link_auto / link_explicit / pin_auto / pin_explicit) onto view-layer 'pin'. Doc: docs_for_ai/mainline-spec-v0.1-rc4-patch.md gains §Patch 7 motivating the rename, the compatibility plan, and the v0.2 deprecation removal milestone.
summary.why: User-facing rename driven by language analysis: 'reconcile' implies bidirectional fixing of inconsistency (system is broken, I must repair) but the operation is in fact unidirectional (a commit on main exists; tag the intent to it) and routine (every GitHub web-UI merge needs it). 'pin' captures the spatial/attachment semantics — a note pinned to a commit — without implying repair. Considered link / acknowledge / anchor / attach; pin won on concreteness and absence of overlap with the existing 'anchoring commit' term in rc2/rc3. The rename is user-surface only: git note history on origin is immutable, so old 'reconcile_*' via values stay as semantic fossils and normaliseVia keeps the view layer stable.
summary.user_goal: Stop calling routine PR-link operations 'reconcile'; teach users a name that matches what the command actually does (pin an intent to a commit).

decisions.rationale: More concrete (spatial), matches the 'note attached to commit' mental model, no collision with git terminology. link is a fine second choice but 'link X to Y' needs both objects in the prompt; 'pin X to Y' is more natural English.
rejected: link — too generic; 'mainline link' begs the question 'link to what?'
anchor — collides with the existing 'anchoring commit' term used in rc2 spec
acknowledge / ack — conveys approval, not attachment
attach — closest to the actual git-notes mechanism, but wordier

decisions.point: Rewriting historical notes on the ref
decisions.chose: Don't — let 'reconcile_auto' values stay in git history forever
decisions.rationale: Notes on origin are pushed; rewriting requires a force-push to the notes ref which is operationally hostile to teams that have already fetched. normaliseVia in sync handles every historical spelling, so the user-visible state is identical.

decisions.chose: Keep as deprecated wrappers in v0.1; remove in v0.2
decisions.rationale: Single-release alias window matches the cobra Deprecated: warning users will see; gives one cycle for downstream callers (and any AGENTS.md text) to migrate.

decisions.chose: ReconcileResult kept as a distinct struct (preserves JSON `reconciled` key); ReconciledLink as Go type alias to PinnedCommit (no JSON shape change)
decisions.rationale: ReconciledLink is a one-row description; renaming it doesn't change its JSON shape so type alias is enough. ReconcileResult has a Reconciled int field whose JSON key external scripts may already parse — kept as a real type with the legacy field name to preserve their behaviour.

risks: Two visible command names in --help is inferior to one. cobra's Hidden+Deprecated puts the alias out of sight in the default help and fires a warning when used, which is the best mainline can do without the alias being a hard removal. Removal lands in v0.2.
Any AGENTS.md / README copy still telling agents to 'mainline reconcile' will see the deprecation warning at runtime; that is by design and the warning explicitly points at the spec patch.
followups: Update AGENTS.md when the patch lands (still references 'mainline merge' / 'mainline revert' but not reconcile, so likely no change needed; verify on dogfood).
Remove Reconcile / ReconcileManual / ReconcileResult / reconcileCmd in v0.2.
Future name audits: 'thread' has the same 'name doesn't quite match function' smell flagged earlier in the dogfood — separate PR.
Spec README front-matter should announce the rename so external readers landing at the top see 'pin' first; deferred to a docs sweep.

architectural_claims: User-surface rename only: data-layer historical via values stay as 'reconcile_*'; normaliseVia bridges new and old to a single view-layer 'pin'.
Backward compatibility for one release: Reconcile / ReconcileManual / ReconcileResult / reconcileCmd are present but Deprecated.
behavioral_changes: mainline pin is the primary command; mainline reconcile prints a deprecation warning and forwards to pin.
New CommitNote writes use via=pin_auto / pin_explicit.

api_changes.signature: engine.(*Service).Reconcile / ReconcileManual marked Deprecated, forward to Pin / PinExplicit

data_model_changes.name: domain CommitNote.via accepts pin_auto / pin_explicit
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Older via values continue to parse via normaliseVia.

migration_notes: Existing notes on the mainline notes ref keep their reconcile_* via values forever; they parse correctly. New writes use pin_*.
